blocks:
Sūrat Wash-Shams

Meccan. Its number of verses is sixteen verses. The Meccan and the first Medinan [schools count] fifteen. Among the rest, the point of difference is the verse فَعَقَرُوها [which is counted by] the Meccan and the first Medinan [schools]. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** وَالشَّمْسِ وَضُحاها ** (1) By the sun and its forenoon brightness, (The) extension of its light, its spreading, and its illumination.

(٢)** وَالْقَمَرِ إِذا تَلاها ** (2) and the moon when it follows it, (It) rises at its setting, taking from its light.

(٣)** وَالنَّهارِ إِذا جَلَّاها ** (3) and the day when it unveils it, At its spreading.

(٤)** وَاللَّيْلِ إِذا يَغْشاها ** (4) and the night when it covers it, So the horizons darken and it clothes them in its blackness.

In al-Kāfī and al-Qummī, narrated from Imam al-Sādiq (peace be upon him), He said: الشَّمْسِ (The Sun) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, through whom Allah clarified their religion for the people. وَالْقَمَرِ (And the Moon) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who followed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He breathed knowledge into him, a breathing (of knowledge). وَاللَّيْلِ (And the Night) are the Imams of tyranny (أئمّة الجور) who seized authority (استبدّوا بالأمر) instead of the Family of the Messenger (آل الرسول), and they sat in a position (مجلساً) to which the Family of the Messenger were more entitled than them, so they covered the religion of Allah with injustice (الظلم) and tyranny (الجور).

(٥) وَالسَّماءِ وَما بَناها ** (5) by the heaven and Him who built it, And the All-Powerful One Who built it. (٦) وَالْأَرْضِ وَما طَحاها ** (6) by the earth and Him who spread it, And the Maker Who spread it (دَحَاهَا). (٧) وَنَفْسٍ وَما سَوَّاها ** (7) by the soul and Him who proportioned it, And the Creator Who سَوَّاها ** (proportioned it), meaning: He perfected its creation. Al-Qummi said: He created it and fashioned it. (٨) فَأَلْهَمَها فُجُورَها وَتَقْواها ** (8) and inspired it with its viciousness and its piety: He said: meaning, He made it know (عَرَّفَهَا) and inspired it, then He gave it choice, and it chose. And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: He made clear to us what we should do and what we should abandon. (٩) قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَنْ زَكَّاها. (9) one who purifies it is felicitous, (١٠) وَقَدْ خابَ مَنْ دَسَّاها. (10) and one who corrupts it is ruinous. In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them), similar to what is in al-Kafi, and he added: قَدْ أَفْلَحَ ** (is felicitous) is he who obeys, and وَقَدْ خابَ ** (is ruinous) is he who disobeys. And Al-Qummi said: مَنْ زَكَّاها ** (one who purifies it) means his soul, he purified it; and وَمَنْ دَسَّاها ** (and one who corrupts it) means he led it astray (أَغْوَاهَا). And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding مَنْ زَكَّاها ** (one who purifies it), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) is the one whose Lord purified him. Regarding مَنْ دَسَّاها ** (and one who corrupts it), he said: It refers to the First and the Second (الأَوَّل وَالثَّانِي) in their pledge of allegiance to him, when he wiped his hand over his palm. It is said that قَدْ أَفْلَحَ ** (one who purifies it is felicitous) is the answer (جَوَاب) to the oath, and the lām (لَام) was omitted due to the length (of the intervening clauses). And it is said that rather, He digressed by mentioning the states of the soul, and the answer (to the oath) is omitted, its estimation being: Allah will destroy the disbelievers of Mecca for their denial of His Messenger, just as He destroyed Thamūd for their denial of Salih.

(١١) كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِطَغْواها ** (11) Thamūd denied [the truth] because of their rebellion, because of their transgression (بِسَبَبِ طُغْيَانِهَا). al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: He says that the transgression (الطغيان) led them to the denial (التكذيب). (١٢) إِذِ انْبَعَثَ أَشْقاها ** (12) when the most wretched among them rose up. The most wretched of Thamūd, who is Qudār ibn Sālif. al-Qummi said: He is the one who hamstrung the she-camel (الناقة). In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He said to Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him): "Who is the most wretched of the former people (الأوّلين)?" He said: "The one who hamstrung the she-camel (عاقر الناقة)." He said: "You have spoken the truth. So who is the most wretched of the latter people (الآخرين)?" He said: "I do not know, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "The one who strikes you upon this," and He pointed to his crown (يافوخه).

(١٣) ** فَقالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللهِ ** صالح **ناقَةَ اللهِ ** (13) Then the apostle of Allah [Salih] said to them, 'Allah's she-camel, and [let her drink] her share of the water!'

[He is] Salih ناقَةَ اللهِ ** (Allah's she-camel), meaning: Leave Allah's she-camel alone and beware of hamstringing her وَسُقْياها ** (and her share of the water), so do not keep her away from it.

(١٤) ** فَكَذَّبُوهُ ** فيما حذرهم من حلول العذاب ان فعلوا فَعَقَرُوها فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ ** فأطبق عليهم العذاب بِذَنْبِهِمْ بسببه فَسَوَّاها ** فسوّى الدّمدمة فلم يفلت منها صغير ولا كبير (14) But they belied him and hamstrung her. So their Lord destroyed them for their sin, and leveled it.

The Almighty's saying: ** فَكَذَّبُوهُ ** (But they belied him) regarding what he warned them about the descent of the punishment if they acted [against the she-camel] فَعَقَرُوها فَدَمْدَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ ** (and hamstrung her. So their Lord destroyed them), meaning: He brought the punishment down upon them completely بِذَنْبِهِمْ (for their sin) because of it فَسَوَّاها ** (and leveled it), meaning: He leveled the destruction (الدّمدمة) so that neither small nor great escaped from it.

Al-Qummi said: He seized them suddenly and unawares during the night.

(١٥) ** وَلا يَخافُ عُقْباها ** (and He does not fear its consequence.) It was said: meaning the consequence (عاقبة) of the utter destruction (الدّمدمة), so that some remnant remains (فيبقى بعض الإبقاء). And the wāw (الواو) is for the state (للحال). And al-Qummi said: Those whom We destroyed do not fear (the consequence) after these people. And it was recited (وقرئ) as فلا يخاف. And he narrated it in al-Majma' (المجمع) from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). He said: And likewise, it is in the mushafs (مصاحف) of the people of Medina (المدينة) and Syria (الشام).

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever reads Wa al-Shams (والشمس), Wa al-Layl (والليل), Wa al-Duha (والضحى), and Alam Nashrah (أ لم نشرح) frequently in a day or a night, nothing that is present with him will remain except that it will bear witness for him on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), even his hair (شعره), his skin (بشره), his flesh (لحمه), his blood (دمه), his veins (عروقه), his sinews (عصبه), his bones (عظامه), and everything that the earth carries of him. And the Lord, Blessed and Exalted is He (تبارك وتعالى), will say: "I have accepted your testimony for My servant and I have permitted it for him. Take him to My Gardens (جنّاتي) so that he may choose from them wherever he desires. Then give it to him without obligation (منّ), but as a mercy from Me and a favor (فضلاً). And congratulations (هنيئاً) to My servant."
